input.content:
<#
.SYNOPSIS
  End-to-end harness: runs every test case through the GitHub Actions
  workflow under nektos/act and verifies exact label output.

.DESCRIPTION
  The workflow exposes three matrix cases that each invoke
  Get-PRLabels.ps1 against a fixture and assert the output JSON matches
  an expected literal. Running `act push --rm` once exercises all three
  cases plus the Pester job in a single act invocation, which keeps us
  inside the 3-act-push budget.

  After act exits, this harness:
    - saves combined stdout+stderr to act-result.txt (overwriting any
      prior file)
    - asserts act exit code 0
    - parses each `CASE_RESULT case=… observed=… expected=…` line and
      asserts `observed == expected` for the three cases
    - asserts every job ended with `Job succeeded`
    - validates the workflow structure (parsed text checks) and that
      actionlint passes
#>
[CmdletBinding()]
param()

$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
Set-StrictMode -Version Latest

$ProjectRoot = $PSScriptRoot
$ResultFile  = Join-Path $ProjectRoot 'act-result.txt'

# ----------------- workflow structure tests (no act) -----------------

function Test-WorkflowStructure {
    Write-Host "::group::Workflow structure tests"
    $wfPath = Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot '.github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml'

    if (-not (Test-Path $wfPath)) { throw "Workflow file missing: $wfPath" }
    Write-Host "  [+] workflow file exists"

    $raw = Get-Content -Raw -Path $wfPath

    foreach ($trigger in @('push:', 'pull_request:', 'workflow_dispatch:')) {
        if ($raw -notmatch [regex]::Escape($trigger)) {
            throw "Workflow missing expected trigger: $trigger"
        }
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] triggers (push, pull_request, workflow_dispatch) present"

    foreach ($job in @('test:', 'assign-labels:')) {
        if ($raw -notmatch [regex]::Escape($job)) {
            throw "Workflow missing expected job: $job"
        }
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] jobs (test, assign-labels) present"

    if ($raw -notmatch 'Get-PRLabels\.ps1') {
        throw "Workflow does not reference Get-PRLabels.ps1"
    }
    if (-not (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot 'Get-PRLabels.ps1'))) {
        throw "Get-PRLabels.ps1 referenced by workflow does not exist"
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] workflow references Get-PRLabels.ps1 (file exists)"

    foreach ($fx in @(
            'fixtures/case-multi-file-union.txt',
            'fixtures/case-empty.txt',
            'fixtures/case-priority.txt')) {
        if (-not (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot $fx))) {
            throw "Fixture referenced by workflow missing on disk: $fx"
        }
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] all matrix fixtures exist"

    if ($raw -notmatch 'actions/checkout@v4') {
        throw "Workflow should use actions/checkout@v4"
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] uses actions/checkout@v4"

    if ($raw -notmatch 'shell:\s*pwsh') {
        throw "Workflow should use 'shell: pwsh' for run steps"
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] uses shell: pwsh"
    Write-Host "::endgroup::"
}

function Test-Actionlint {
    Write-Host "::group::actionlint validation"
    Push-Location $script:ProjectRoot
    try {
        $output = & actionlint .github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml 2>&1 | Out-String
        $code = $LASTEXITCODE
        Write-Host $output
        if ($code -ne 0) { throw "actionlint failed with exit $code" }
        Write-Host "  [+] actionlint exit 0"
    } finally {
        Pop-Location
    }
    Write-Host "::endgroup::"
}

# ----------------- act run -----------------

# Each (case, expectedJson) pair MUST match the matrix in the workflow.
$Cases = @(
    @{ Name = 'multi-file-union'; Expected = '["api","ci","documentation","source","tests"]' },
    @{ Name = 'empty-changeset';  Expected = '[]' },
    @{ Name = 'priority-mode';    Expected = '["api","documentation","source"]' }
)

function Invoke-AllCasesViaAct {
    Write-Host "::group::act push (single invocation, all matrix cases)"
    Push-Location $script:ProjectRoot
    try {
        Write-Host "  Running: act push --rm"
        $combined = & act push --rm 2>&1 | Out-String
        $exitCode = $LASTEXITCODE
    } finally {
        Pop-Location
    }
    Write-Host "::endgroup::"

    $header = @"
# act-result.txt — generated by run-act-tests.ps1
# act push exit code: $exitCode

==============================================================================
=== act push --rm (single invocation, all matrix cases)
==============================================================================
"@
    Set-Content -Path $script:ResultFile -Value $header
    Add-Content -Path $script:ResultFile -Value $combined

    if ($exitCode -ne 0) {
        throw "act exited with $exitCode (expected 0). See $script:ResultFile."
    }
    Write-Host "[+] act exit code 0"

    # Per-case label assertions. The workflow prints exactly one
    # `CASE_RESULT case=NAME observed=JSON expected=JSON` line per matrix
    # leg.
    $lines = $combined -split "`r?`n"
    foreach ($case in $script:Cases) {
        $name = $case.Name
        $expected = $case.Expected
        $hit = $lines | Where-Object { $_ -match "CASE_RESULT case=$([regex]::Escape($name))\b" } | Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $hit) {
            throw "No CASE_RESULT line for case '$name'."
        }
        if ($hit -notmatch "observed=(\S+)\s+expected=(\S+)") {
            throw "Could not parse CASE_RESULT for '$name': $hit"
        }
        $observed = $Matches[1]
        $expFound = $Matches[2]
        if ($expFound -ne $expected) {
            throw "[$name] expected literal mismatch — workflow says '$expFound', harness expects '$expected'."
        }
        if ($observed -ne $expected) {
            throw "[$name] label mismatch — observed=$observed expected=$expected"
        }
        Write-Host "[+] case '$name' labels=$observed (matches expected)"
    }

    # Job-success assertions. The matrix expands to N legs of
    # 'assign-labels' plus the 'test' job, so we expect at least 4 lines.
    $succeededCount = ([regex]::Matches($combined, 'Job succeeded')).Count
    $expectedJobs = $script:Cases.Count + 1   # matrix legs + Pester job
    if ($succeededCount -lt $expectedJobs) {
        throw "Expected at least $expectedJobs 'Job succeeded' lines (Pester + $($script:Cases.Count) matrix legs); got $succeededCount."
    }
    Write-Host "[+] $succeededCount jobs reported 'Job succeeded' (>= $expectedJobs required)"
}

# ============================================================
# Run
# ============================================================

Test-WorkflowStructure
Test-Actionlint
Invoke-AllCasesViaAct

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=========================================="
Write-Host "ALL TESTS PASSED."
Write-Host "Results saved to: $script:ResultFile"
Write-Host "=========================================="

<#
.SYNOPSIS
  Runs the GitHub Actions workflow under nektos/act for a set of test
  cases. For each case it:
    1. Stages a temp git repo with the project files plus the case's
       changed-files fixture.
    2. Runs `act push --rm` (optionally passing CONFLICT_RESOLUTION env).
    3. Captures stdout+stderr, appends it (with a delimiter) to
       act-result.txt in the project root.
    4. Asserts act exited 0, every job ended with "Job succeeded", and
       the LABELS_JSON line matches the case's expected JSON exactly.
    5. Also asserts workflow structure (parsed YAML) and that
       actionlint passes.

  Capped at 3 act push runs by design — one per case.
#>
[CmdletBinding()]
param()

$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
Set-StrictMode -Version Latest

$ProjectRoot = $PSScriptRoot
$ResultFile  = Join-Path $ProjectRoot 'act-result.txt'

# Reset the captured-output file at the start of each harness run so
# stale lines from a prior run can't satisfy a current assertion.
Set-Content -Path $ResultFile -Value "# act-result.txt — generated by run-act-tests.ps1`n" -NoNewline

function Write-Section {
    param([string] $Title)
    $bar = '=' * 78
    Add-Content -Path $script:ResultFile -Value "`n$bar`n=== $Title`n$bar`n"
}

function Invoke-ActCase {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string] $Name,
        [Parameter(Mandatory)][AllowEmptyCollection()][string[]] $ChangedFiles,
        [Parameter(Mandatory)][AllowEmptyCollection()][string[]] $ExpectedLabels,  # already sorted
        [string] $ConflictResolution = 'Union'
    )

    Write-Host "::group::act case: $Name"
    Write-Host "  Conflict mode : $ConflictResolution"
    Write-Host "  Changed files :"
    $ChangedFiles | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "    $_" }
    Write-Host "  Expected      : $($ExpectedLabels -join ', ')"

    $tmp = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) "prlabels-act-$([Guid]::NewGuid())"
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $tmp -Force | Out-Null

    try {
        # Copy project files into the temp repo (skip the harness's own
        # output file plus any pre-existing .git/temp dirs).
        Get-ChildItem -Path $script:ProjectRoot -Force |
            Where-Object { $_.Name -notin @('.git', 'act-result.txt') } |
            ForEach-Object { Copy-Item -Path $_.FullName -Destination $tmp -Recurse -Force }

        # Override the committed fixture with the case-specific list.
        $fixturePath = Join-Path $tmp 'fixtures/changed-files.txt'
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $fixturePath) -Force | Out-Null
        $ChangedFiles -join "`n" | Set-Content -Path $fixturePath

        Push-Location $tmp
        try {
            git init --quiet --initial-branch=main 2>&1 | Out-Null
            git config user.email 'harness@example.com'
            git config user.name  'Harness'
            git add . | Out-Null
            git commit -m "case: $Name" --quiet | Out-Null

            $actArgs = @('push', '--rm')
            if ($ConflictResolution -ne 'Union') {
                $actArgs += @('--env', "CONFLICT_RESOLUTION=$ConflictResolution")
            }

            Write-Host "  Running: act $($actArgs -join ' ')"
            $combined = & act @actArgs 2>&1 | Out-String
            $exitCode = $LASTEXITCODE
        }
        finally {
            Pop-Location
        }

        Write-Section -Title "case: $Name (exit=$exitCode, conflict=$ConflictResolution)"
        Add-Content -Path $script:ResultFile -Value $combined

        # ---------------- assertions ----------------
        if ($exitCode -ne 0) {
            throw "[$Name] act exited with $exitCode (expected 0)."
        }

        # Each job must end with "Job succeeded". act prints a per-job
        # line of the form `[Workflow / Job] ... Job succeeded`.
        $succeededLines = ([regex]::Matches($combined, 'Job succeeded'))
        if ($succeededLines.Count -lt 2) {
            throw "[$Name] expected at least 2 'Job succeeded' lines (one per job); got $($succeededLines.Count)."
        }

        # Pull the LABELS_JSON line from the assigner step. The leading
        # `[…] ` prefix that act adds is stripped.
        $jsonLine = ($combined -split "`n") |
            Where-Object { $_ -match 'LABELS_JSON=' } |
            Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $jsonLine) {
            throw "[$Name] no LABELS_JSON= line found in act output."
        }
        $jsonText = ($jsonLine -replace '^.*LABELS_JSON=', '').Trim()
        $labels = $jsonText | ConvertFrom-Json
        $observed = @($labels) | Sort-Object

        # Compare arrays as canonicalised strings to side-step nullish
        # quirks when both arrays are empty.
        $obsKey = ($observed -join '|')
        $expKey = ($ExpectedLabels -join '|')
        if ($obsKey -ne $expKey) {
            throw "[$Name] label mismatch.`n  expected: [$($ExpectedLabels -join ', ')]`n  observed: [$($observed -join ', ')]"
        }

        Write-Host "  PASS — labels match: [$($observed -join ', ')]"
    }
    finally {
        if (Test-Path $tmp) { Remove-Item -Path $tmp -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
        Write-Host "::endgroup::"
    }
}

# ----- Workflow structure tests (no act required) -----

function Test-WorkflowStructure {
    Write-Host "::group::Workflow structure tests"
    $wfPath = Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot '.github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml'

    if (-not (Test-Path $wfPath)) {
        throw "Workflow file missing: $wfPath"
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] workflow file exists"

    $raw = Get-Content -Raw -Path $wfPath

    # Trigger checks (string-level — robust without a YAML parser dep).
    foreach ($trigger in @('push:', 'pull_request:', 'workflow_dispatch:')) {
        if ($raw -notmatch [regex]::Escape($trigger)) {
            throw "Workflow missing expected trigger: $trigger"
        }
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] triggers (push, pull_request, workflow_dispatch) present"

    # Job checks.
    foreach ($job in @('test:', 'assign-labels:')) {
        if ($raw -notmatch [regex]::Escape($job)) {
            throw "Workflow missing expected job: $job"
        }
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] jobs (test, assign-labels) present"

    # Script reference.
    if ($raw -notmatch 'Get-PRLabels\.ps1') {
        throw "Workflow does not reference Get-PRLabels.ps1"
    }
    if (-not (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:ProjectRoot 'Get-PRLabels.ps1'))) {
        throw "Get-PRLabels.ps1 referenced by workflow does not exist on disk"
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] workflow references Get-PRLabels.ps1 (file exists)"

    if ($raw -notmatch 'actions/checkout@v4') {
        throw "Workflow should use actions/checkout@v4"
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] uses actions/checkout@v4"

    if ($raw -notmatch 'shell:\s*pwsh') {
        throw "Workflow should use 'shell: pwsh' for run steps"
    }
    Write-Host "  [+] uses shell: pwsh"
    Write-Host "::endgroup::"
}

function Test-Actionlint {
    Write-Host "::group::actionlint validation"
    Push-Location $script:ProjectRoot
    try {
        $output = & actionlint .github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml 2>&1 | Out-String
        $code = $LASTEXITCODE
        Write-Host $output
        if ($code -ne 0) {
            throw "actionlint failed with exit $code"
        }
        Write-Host "  [+] actionlint exit 0"
    } finally {
        Pop-Location
    }
    Write-Host "::endgroup::"
}

# ============================================================
# Drive the suite
# ============================================================

Test-WorkflowStructure
Test-Actionlint

# Three act cases — one push run each, the full budget.
Invoke-ActCase `
    -Name 'multi-file-union' `
    -ChangedFiles @(
        'docs/readme.md',
        'src/api/users.ps1',
        'src/lib/utils.ps1',
        'foo.test.ps1',
        '.github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml'
    ) `
    -ExpectedLabels @('api', 'ci', 'documentation', 'source', 'tests') `
    -ConflictResolution 'Union'

Invoke-ActCase `
    -Name 'empty-changeset' `
    -ChangedFiles @() `
